title: Image Size, Build Performance, Runtime Efficiency, and Reproducibility · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective measured build and runtime optimization experiment; isolate the first causal fault in image size, build performance, runtime efficiency, and reproducibility and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in measured build and runtime optimization experiment and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pr…
objectives: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.
Measure cold, warm, changed-source, changed-lockfile, and remote-cache builds with plain progress and controlled inputs.
Compare trusted base, distroless or scratch, packages, debugging needs, certificates, locales, dynamic libraries, and patch workflow.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Image Size Layer Analysis
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team compares a warm local build with a cold CI build and blames a large final image, while context transfer and cross-platform cache dominate. Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.

Inspect the Docker Image Size Layer Analysis trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Image Size Layer Analysis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Image Size Layer Analysis, keep Docker Image Size Layer Analysis connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Docker Image Size Layer Analysis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Image Size Layer Analysis, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Image Size Layer Analysis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Measure each stage and compare controlled output digests. Competency probe: Measure compressed, unpacked, shared, unique, and writable size and trace which layer, package, or artifact owns the change.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Image Size Layer Analysis, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: The docker images SIZE column is the exact additional disk and network cost for every host.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Reproducible Build Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-w30w60". Tailor its build-performance-budget.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Reproducible Build Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Reproducible Build Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Reproducible Build Evidence, keep Docker Reproducible Build Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-reproducible-build-evidence-w30w60
  - competency: docker-reproducible-build-evidence
    artifact: build-performance-budget.yaml
    decision: reproducible build evidence
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: production-like canary with read-only application state
    fault-injection: restore a volume under a different numeric user identity
    retained-proof: attached SBOM provenance signature and policy decision
    case-key: w30w60
    change: |
      context-bytes: measured
      cache-hit-rate: observed-not-assumed
      cold-build: recorded
      image-bytes: per-platform
      runtime-budget: cpu-memory-io
      reproducibility: digest-compared
    verify: docker buildx du && docker image inspect candidate --format json
    failure: Reject image size as the sole performance metric, warm-cache timing as a cold build, or equal source commits as proof of equal image digests.
    repair: Measure context, transfer, layers, cache, startup, CPU, memory, and I/O separately; change one variable and compare digests under controlled inputs.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Reproducible Build Evidence structural check is only one gate. measured build and runtime optimization experiment must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
